problem.title: Design Tic-Tac-Toe
problem.description: Design a Tic-Tac-Toe board of size n×n. Player 1 plays 'X' and player 2 plays 'O'. Implement a TicTacToe class that supports move(row, col, player) which places a piece and returns the winner (1 or 2) if the current player wins, or 0 if no one has won yet. You may assume each move is valid.
assumptions: Each move is guaranteed to be valid (within bounds and on an empty cell).
Once a winner is declared no further moves occur.

example.explanation: Player 1 has pieces on the main diagonal: (0,0),(1,1),(2,2). All three match, so player 1 wins.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Row/Column/Diagonal Counters
solutions.description: Instead of scanning the full board after each move (O(n) per move), maintain an integer count per row, per column, and for the two diagonals for each player. When player p places at (row, col), increment their row counter, column counter, and diagonal counters if applicable. If any counter reaches n, player p wins. Use +1 for player 1 and -1 for player 2 in shared arrays to halve the storage, or keep separate arrays for clarity. Each move is O(1) and total space is O(n).
solutions.code_python:
class TicTacToe:
    def __init__(self, n: int) -> None:
        self.n = n
        self.rows = [0] * n
        self.cols = [0] * n
        self.diag = 0
        self.anti_diag = 0

    def move(self, row: int, col: int, player: int) -> int:
        delta = 1 if player == 1 else -1
        self.rows[row] += delta
        self.cols[col] += delta
        if row == col:
            self.diag += delta
        if row + col == self.n - 1:
            self.anti_diag += delta
        n = self.n
        if (abs(self.rows[row]) == n or abs(self.cols[col]) == n
                or abs(self.diag) == n or abs(self.anti_diag) == n):
            return player
        return 0

complexity.space: O(n) for row and column arrays

example_walkthrough.text: n=3. Player 1 uses +1, player 2 uses -1. move(0,0,1): rows[0]=1, cols[0]=1, diag=1. move(0,2,2): rows[0]=0, cols[2]=-1, anti_diag=-1. move(1,1,1): rows[1]=1, cols[1]=1, diag=2, anti_diag=0. move(2,0,2): rows[2]=-1, cols[0]=0, anti_diag=-1. move(2,2,1): rows[2]=0, cols[2]=0, diag=3 → |diag|==3==n, return player 1.
